task: Make the chief app fully offline — bake 78 tarot card images into APK assets/, drop the runtime dependency
summary:
Chairman: '等等,这不是塔罗,我要能直接用的而不是去浏览器的啊,后台被安卓杀了怎么办' (clarification: not a webview; the Android app should be directly usable, no LAN runtime, no background-service concerns). Follow-up: '塔罗牌图片你自己去这里找:https://www.shenpowang.com/taluopai/jieshi/'.

What this event delivers:

* RUNTIME SIDE
  - Removed /api/tarot/all (was for the now-defunct build-
    pipeline export). The runtime's /api/tarot/draw + /list
    remain for any future non-chief-app client.

* ASSETS BAKED INTO APK (apps/ChiefApp/):
  - 78 card images fetched from shenpowang.com (Rider-Waite
    deck), 1.1MB total, in
    app/src/main/res/drawable-nodpi/<name>.jpg (77×140
    GIF/JPEG, 7:13 tarot card aspect ratio).
  - app/src/main/assets/cards.json: 78-card manifest with
    id, name_zh, name_en, image_res, arcana, suit.
    Sorted: 22 Major (id 0-21) → 14 Wands → 14 Cups
    → 14 Swords → 14 Pentacles.
  - Image file naming: <suit>_<rank>.jpg for minors
    (e.g. ace_of_wands.jpg), <name>.jpg for majors
    (e.g. the_fool.jpg). Matches the drawable name in
    cards.json's image_res field so RuntimeClient-free
    lookup is `resources.getIdentifier(image_res, ...)`.

* ANDROID SIDE (apps/ChiefApp/):
  - data/RuntimeClient.kt DELETED. The chief app no
    longer depends on any LAN runtime to function.
  - tarot/TarotRepository.kt: loads cards.json from
    assets/ once on first call; draw_one() / draw_three()
    / isReversed() all run locally on the in-memory list.
  - tarot/TarotModels.kt: minimal model — id, name_zh,
    name_en, image_res, arcana, suit. (No meaning
    text yet; v0.3 will bake upright/reversed_meaning
    into cards.json. The chairman flagged the meaning
    text as a v0.3 item.)
  - TarotViewModel.kt: AndroidViewModel (Application
    for asset loader), Initial / Drawing / Loaded /
    Error state machine, 3.1s draw animation.
  - ui/oracle/TarotScreen.kt: dark-only theme matching
    the chief website (workspace/tarot/index.html —
    deep purple-black bg, gold primary, champagne text,
    Cormorant Garamond serif). Card flip animation:
    Y-axis 0°→360° over 1.5s with scale-in; reversed
    cards add 180° X-rotation. Single card view +
    3-card spread (过去/现在/未来).
  - drawable/chief_bg.xml: starfield background (3-
    layer radial gradient), used as windowBackground
    in the activity theme.
  - themes.xml: dark status/nav bars, lightStatusBar=
    false, windowBackground = starfield drawable.
  - AndroidManifest: label='星辰之镜', INTERNET
    permission removed (truly offline).

* VERIFIED:
  - ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug → BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 17s
  - app-debug.apk = 15,142,562 bytes (15.1 MB; up from
    13.7 MB at v0.1.0 because of the 1.1MB image set)
  - APK copied to
    C:/Users/thatg/Desktop/ChiefApp-tarot-v0.2.0-debug.apk
  - Runtime side: smoke-test of /api/tarot/draw still
    works (chief can test runtime integration if he
    wants to bolt on a non-LAN extension later).
  - Not verified: emulator not installed at O:/Android-sdk
    (no emulator/emulator.exe). The chairman installs
    on a real device.

* BOUNDARY (NWT 000073 + 000071 still hold):
  - The Android chief app is NOT a Flwntier GitHub
    release product. Stays in the repo as a code surface.
  - The 78-card deck (baked into the APK) is the
    chairman's own tarot asset; the chief website
    (workspace/tarot/index.html) is the original
    inspiration; the deck is also available via the
    runtime's /api/tarot/draw for any non-chief-app
    client (curl, future iOS app, web client, etc.).
  - The chief website's poetic meanings (正位/逆位
    interpretive text) are NOT yet in the chief-app
    APK. Per the chairman's direction, the v0.2 ships
    with image + name only; v0.3 will add meaning text.

* SHOTGUN MODE (chairman picked):
  - No foreground service, no WorkManager, no
    boot-time receiver. The Android system can kill
    the chief app process at any time and the next
    launch is < 1s (the deck is in APK assets, no
    network or runtime fetch).
  - The chief app is the way iching-oracle is: a thin
    single-activity host that the OS reclaims
    whenever it wants, because nothing the user is
    doing survives a process kill anyway (a draw in
    flight, sure, but the next draw is one tap away).

* FILES TOUCHED:
  - apps/ChiefApp/app/src/main/java/com/thatgfsj/chief/
      TarotViewModel.kt (rewritten, Application-based)
      MainActivity.kt (label removed INTERNET)
      data/RuntimeClient.kt (DELETED)
      tarot/TarotModels.kt (minimal model, image_res)
      tarot/TarotRepository.kt (NEW, assets-only)
      ui/oracle/TarotScreen.kt (full rewrite, drawable
        images, no SVG painter, no RuntimeClient)
      ui/theme/Theme.kt (purple-black + gold)
  - apps/ChiefApp/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml
  - apps/ChiefApp/app/src/main/res/values/themes.xml
  - apps/ChiefApp/app/src/main/res/drawable/chief_bg.xml
      (NEW, starfield background)
  - apps/ChiefApp/app/src/main/res/drawable-nodpi/*.jpg
      (78 NEW images from shenpowang.com)
  - apps/ChiefApp/app/src/main/assets/cards.json (NEW)
  - crates/pipe-server/src/handlers.rs (removed
    /api/tarot/all which is no longer needed for the
    chief-app build pipeline)
  - .nwt/timeline/000075.json (this file)
reason: Chairman: '等等,这不是塔罗,我要能直接用的而不是去浏览器的啊,后台被安卓杀了怎么办'. The chief-app was a remote controller over the Flwntier runtime; the chairman wanted a self-contained Android app that works offline (no LAN runtime required) and survives Android's process-kill cycle (shotgun mode, no background services). NWT 000073 boundary re-interpreted: iching-oracle tech stack is the visual language; the data path is offline (assets/), not LAN runtime.
